channel.title: NYT > Technology
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: UK Institute Is Hunting for Dangers Lurking in AI
item.description: The government’s A.I. Security Institute, staffed by alumni from OpenAI and Google, is becoming a model for countries grappling with A.I.’s emerging risks.
item.dc:creator: Adam Satariano and Paul Mozur
item.media:credit: Jeremie Souteyrat for The New York Times
item.media:description: Along Parliament Square in London is the home of the A.I. Security Institute, which employs weapons inspectors, epidemiologists and code breakers to probe the technology’s risks.

item.title: One Job That Is Growing in the A.I. Era? Cybersecurity Experts.
item.description: Demand for security engineers has surged as artificial intelligence generates a glut of new code and models like Anthropic’s Mythos create new concerns.
item.dc:creator: Kate Conger

item.title: Our Field Trip to Google I/O + A Sit-Down With Sundar Pichai + System Update
item.description: “This is the only recent gathering of a large number of people where mentions of A.I. did not produce a large chorus of boos.”
item.dc:creator: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Rachel Cohn, Whitney Jones, Vjeran Pavic, Katie McMurran, Dan Powell, Elisheba Ittoop, Rowan Niemisto and Alyssa Moxley
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by The New York Times; Photo by Karl Mondon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Sundar Pichai Understands Why People Are Anxious About A.I.
item.description: After a busy Google I/O, the company’s chief executive sits down with the hosts of “Hard Fork” to discuss the future of Google Search, how he’s using A.I. agents and his advice for college graduates.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton and Whitney Jones
item.media:description: “People, rightfully so, are anxious about the future that this technology will bring,” Google’s chief executive, Sundar Pichai, said this week after the company’s annual developer conference.

item.title: As A.I. Fever Rises in Silicon Valley, Pope Leo Has a Few Words
item.description: The American pope wants to take artificial intelligence down a notch. Is he challenging the tech companies, or will tech take over the papacy?
item.dc:creator: David Streitfeld

item.title: Waymo Suspends Service in Six Cities After Cars Drove Into Flooded Roads
item.description: The temporary shutdowns came after videos emerged showing two Waymo cars stopped on swamped streets in Atlanta on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson
item.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Waymo also announced it was temporarily halting its highway service this week to improve its navigation system.

item.title: Google Appeals Landmark Ruling Declaring It a Monopolist in Search
item.description: Google asked a federal appeals court on Friday to reverse a judge’s ruling that it had violated antitrust laws, as well as a requirement it share some data with rivals.
item.dc:creator: David McCabe
item.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Google campus in Mountain View, Calif.

item.title: ‘Peanuts’ Music Owner Sues Interior Department and 3 Companies for Copyright Infringement
item.description: The owner of the “Peanuts” catalog would really like it if companies and the U.S. government stopped using its music without permission.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Ortiz
item.media:description: A scene from “A Charlie Brown Christmas.”

item.title: Meta Settles a School District’s Social Media Addiction Lawsuit
item.description: Snap, TikTok and YouTube had already settled with the Kentucky district, allowing the companies to avert the first in a series of federal trials.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang
item.media:description: Meta, led by Mark Zuckerberg, shown at the U.S. Capitol in March, lost jury verdicts that month in two similar lawsuits.

item.title: Trump Cancels Signing of Executive Order Granting Oversight of A.I. Models
item.description: The president said he postponed the executive order, which would give the government power to evaluate A.I. models before their release, over concerns about “aspects of it.”
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle and Sheera Frenkel
item.media:description: On Wednesday President Trump invited A.I. chief executives to Thursday’s signing event, but many could not make it in time.

item.title: Gov. Gavin Newsom to Sign Executive Order Aimed at A.I. Job Loss
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom issued an executive order to explore an overhaul of labor policies to deal with potential mass job displacement from artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang
item.media:description: Gov. Gavin Newsom’s mandate is more exploratory in ideas than a law that will immediately change the jobs picture for California residents.

item.title: Samsung Averts a Walkout With Big Bonuses, but Discord Over A.I. Profits Brews
item.description: Samsung Electronics, a global memory chip supplier, has been a major beneficiary of the A.I boom. It has also become the center of a debate over how to divide its spoils.
item.dc:creator: Max Kim
item.media:description: Outside the Samsung Electronics semiconductor plant in Pyeongtaek, South Korea, on Thursday.

item.title: Soundtrack to 8,000 Job Cuts: A Meta Worker’s Layoff-Themed A.I. Songs
item.description: On a dark day of layoffs at Meta, one employee responded by creating an internal radio station that plays songs about job cuts — generated by artificial intelligence, of course.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times

item.title: Musk’s SpaceX Reveals Its Finances for the First Time as It Readies for IPO
item.description: Mr. Musk’s rocket and satellite maker disclosed its financial performance as it prepares to go public in what is set to be one of the largest offerings to date.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Lauren Hirsch
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: A successful SpaceX offering would bring riches to Wall Street, the company’s employees and, of course, Elon Musk, who is already the world’s richest person.

item.title: OpenAI Prepares to File for an I.P.O. in Coming Weeks
item.description: OpenAI would be one of the most highly anticipated potential initial public offerings, in what is set to be a major year of I.P.O.s for Silicon Valley.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac and Cade Metz
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sam Altman, OpenAI’s chief executive. The company is facing challenges from the tech giant Google and an A.I. rival, Anthropic.

item.title: Meta Lays Off 8,000 Employees, as A.I. Casualties Mount
item.description: Employees have signed petitions against being tracked by A.I. and were trying to figure out who had been let go on Wednesday, as the Silicon Valley giant tries to transform into an A.I.-first company.
item.dc:creator: Eli Tan, Kalley Huang and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Ian C. Bates for The New York Times
item.media:description: Meta’s headquarters in Menlo Park, Calif. The company, which renamed itself for the metaverse, is transforming itself for the era of artificial intelligence.

item.title: How Google Is Starting to Win the A.I. Race
item.description: Despite its early stumbles, Google’s Gemini has leapfrogged ChatGPT in relevance and usefulness. Soon, it will be ubiquitous.
item.dc:creator: Brian X. Chen

item.title: Rising Energy Costs and Data Centers at Heart of NextEra’s Dominion Bid
item.description: NextEra’s proposed acquisition of Dominion Energy comes as Americans are paying a lot more for electricity, and data centers are demanding a lot more power from utilities.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn
item.media:credit: Tristan Spinski for The New York Times
item.media:description: NextEra Energy’s acquisition of Dominion Energy would consolidate utility operations along much of the East Coast, which could shape electricity service for tens of millions of customers.

item.title: Senate Republicans Introduce Funding Measure to Combat Online Child Abuse
item.description: As part of the reconciliation bill, the senators have allocated $108 million over three years to add nearly 200 specialists at the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang
item.media:description: Senator Josh Hawley, Republican of Missouri, has championed the reconciliation bill.

item.title: Tesla’s Semi Truck could Jolt the Trucking Industry
item.description: California truckers have expressed strong interest in the Tesla Semi because it costs much less and can travel further on a charge than electric trucks sold by established manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing and Gabriela Bhaskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Screens on either side of a Tesla Semi’s steering wheel provide a view of the traffic around the vehicle.

item.title: As OpenAI Celebrates Court Win Against Musk, Other Challenges Lie Ahead
item.description: A jury’s rejection of Elon Musk’s $150 billion lawsuit against OpenAI was a major hurdle crossed. But the maker of ChatGPT faces a list of other problems.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Natallie Rocha
item.media:description: Lawyers for OpenAI, who won a resounding victory on Monday, outside the federal courthouse in Oakland, Calif.

item.title: Bipartisan Bill Would Impose New Annual Fee on Electric Vehicles
item.description: A House transportation bill introduced this week would require owners of electric cars to pay $130 to cover the cost of road repairs.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times
item.media:description: Congress is considering imposing a new annual fee on electric vehicles to help pay for road repairs.
